summary: FQ-09U2 settlement-responsive stem layering: the adaptive score's vertical layer is live. Opened with the mandated spike, finding RECORDED - an AudioStreamSynchronized group DOES play as a clip inside AudioStreamInteractive in Godot 4.6.1 (live in-smoke probe with nested generated WAV tones); U2 nevertheless ships the parallel LayerPlayer design because the suite has one shared phase-locked stem set, with nesting now a proven option for future increments. The six stems load at runtime, are validated against the exact 53.333s grid length (mismatch disables layering only - context music untouched), and play as an AudioStreamSynchronized bed started from the context player's position in the same frame (phase-aligned by construction). Data-defined mix (stem_mix, validator-enforced): volume targets lerp from settlement resilience (foundation), coherence (hearth), the pressure score (pressure stem with a -16dB storm-texture floor), player attunement ratio, mining/movement activity (motion), and the collapse edge past pressure 0.7 (fracture); volumes move at 6 dB/s and never snap (smoke-verified to the decimal: -40 -> -37.00 over 0.5s). Zero save keys. Suite 226 -> 234 (8 fq09u2_* checks); haiku verifier NO FINDINGS. Concurrent Codex art drop (art/generated/players/, untracked) deliberately left for its own intake run; commits used explicit paths.
scope: FQ-09U2 only per docs/WORK_ORDER_FQ_09U_ADAPTIVE_MUSIC.md - stems and mixing, no stingers, no settings UI, no save-schema or gameplay changes, no M8str0 changes.
next_action: FQ-09U3: stingers over temporary ducking, music/SFX volume settings, pause behavior, final asset validation. Separately: the Codex players-art intake with the player-sprite renderer extension.
